source_pr.title: test: fix unstable test TestStaleReadProcessorWithSelectTable
source_pr.body:
Signed-off-by: YangKeao <yangkeao@chunibyo.icu>

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #37228

Problem Summary:

If the test takes more than 5 seconds to run, it will fail.

### What is changed and how it works?

Extend the staleness in the test. The threshold is now extended to 100 seconds (just a random big number)

symptoms: `TestStaleReadProcessorWithSelectTable` can fail on slow/contended environments because it uses a very small `tidb_read_staleness` window (5 seconds) and assumes the test finishes within that window.
failure_signature: TestStaleReadProcessorWithSelectTable fails when the test runtime exceeds the configured staleness window (timestamp/evaluator mismatch).
root_cause_explanation: The test's staleness window was too small (`@@tidb_read_staleness=-5`, i.e. 5 seconds). If the test takes longer than that due to machine load, the calculated stale-read timestamp and the evaluator result can drift and cause failures. The fix increases the window to 100 seconds (`@@tidb_read_staleness=-100`) and updates expected timestamp calculations accordingly, making the test robust to slower environments.
fix_pattern: Increase `@@tidb_read_staleness` in the test from 5s to 100s and update expected TS calculations to avoid time-window based flakiness.

evidence: evidence: test: fix unstable test TestStaleReadProcessorWithSelectTable
